guarantee: An access token whose backing AuthSession has been revoked (revokedAt set) is rejected by the guards on /me and /resend-verification with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and request.user is never populated.
testTitle: authGuard rejects access tokens whose backing session has been revoked

guarantee: Guards derive request.user (organisationId, role) from the current DB row, not from stale JWT claims, so a token minted with an old org/role cannot act with those stale claims.
testTitle: authGuard uses current database role and organisation instead of stale JWT claims

guarantee: Refresh rotation atomically revokes the presented session and issues exactly one replacement; if the conditional revoke updates 0 rows (lost race), rotation fails with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN and NO replacement AuthSession is created.
testTitle: refresh token rotation fails without creating a replacement session when revoke loses the race

guarantee: Replaying an already-revoked refresh token (theft detection) is rejected with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN AND triggers revocation of all of that user's active sessions, without looking up the user or creating a new session.
testTitle: refresh token replay revokes active sessions for the affected user

guarantee: Access tokens are signed/verified with pinned HS256 and bound to the charitypilot-api issuer and charitypilot-web audience; tokens using a different algorithm, audience, or missing issuer are rejected.
testTitle: access tokens are signed and verified with the pinned HS256 algorithm

guarantee: resetPassword consumes the reset token atomically (conditional updateMany) before revoking all active sessions; a concurrently-consumed token (count 0) fails with 400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN and sessions are NOT revoked.
testTitle: resetPassword consumes the token, sets the password, and revokes all sessions

guarantee: verifyEmail is single-use: a valid token marks the account verified, and a token consumed concurrently (updateMany count 0) fails with 400 INVALID_VERIFY_TOKEN without an unconditional update.
testTitle: verifyEmail marks the account verified when the token is valid

guarantee: On successful POST /login the route sets the auth (access + refresh) cookies via setAuthCookies and returns only publicUser (no passwordHash); a 200 response carries a Set-Cookie header.
testTitle: login route issues auth cookies and returns the public user without secrets

guarantee: Secrets and tokens (authorization header, cookies, body.password, body.refreshToken, accessToken, provider keys) are redacted in production logs and never appear in serialized error output.
testTitle: production logger redacts browser credentials, provider secrets, and auth tokens

guarantee: Each Zod-validated auth endpoint (register, login, forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email) rejects a malformed/missing-field body with HTTP 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and a structured details array, never a 500 or stack leak.
testTitle: auth endpoints reject malformed bodies with a 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never 500

guarantee: register rejects passwords failing the strength policy (min 8, upper/lower/digit) and over-length name/organisationName with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, never starting the org/user-creation transaction.
testTitle: register rejects weak passwords without starting the creation transaction

guarantee: When the email provider fails to send the verification email, POST /resend-verification returns a clean 503 {code:'EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED'} (from resendEmailVerification when sendEmailVerification resolves falsy) rather than a 200 or 500.
testTitle: resendEmailVerification surfaces a 503 when the verification email cannot be sent

guarantee: register does not disclose that an email is already registered: an existing email yields 202 with the neutral REGISTRATION_ACCEPTED_MESSAGE, no Set-Cookie, and no org/user creation transaction is started.
testTitle: register route does not disclose duplicate emails or issue session cookies

guarantee: register also flattens the DB-enforced unique-email race: when the creation transaction throws a Prisma P2002 unique-constraint error, the service returns the same neutral accepted message instead of leaking the conflict or 500ing.
testTitle: register flattens a raced duplicate-email unique-constraint failure to the neutral message

guarantee: forgot-password is enumeration-safe: it returns the same generic message for both known and unknown accounts, storing a hashed (sha256) reset token only for a known account and sending nothing for an unknown one.
testTitle: forgotPassword sends a reset link for a known account and returns the generic message

guarantee: login is enumeration-safe: an unknown email returns the generic 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS ('Invalid email or password') and the no-user path still spends a full bcrypt comparison so latency does not reveal account existence.
testTitle: login returns the generic INVALID_CREDENTIALS error for unknown accounts

guarantee: Sensitive auth endpoints (forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email, resend-verification, refresh) enforce per-route throttling stricter than the global limit, returning 429 on the 6th request within the window.
testTitle: forgot-password has route-specific throttling below the global limit

guarantee: resend-verification throttling is keyed by the caller credential (Authorization bearer token or access cookie, hashed with the caller IP), so repeated attempts with one invalid credential cannot exhaust the same-IP bucket for a different credential.
testTitle: resend-verification throttling is keyed by caller credential

guarantee: Refresh throttling is keyed by the caller and hashed refresh-token identifier from the request body or refresh cookie, so attempts with one invalid refresh token cannot exhaust the same-IP bucket for a different refresh token.
testTitle: refresh throttling is keyed by caller and normalized body identifier

guarantee: Logout throttling is keyed by the caller and hashed refresh-token identifier from the request body or refresh cookie, so repeated logout calls with one refresh token cannot exhaust the same-IP bucket for a different token.
testTitle: logout throttling is keyed by refresh token identifier

guarantee: The two non-public auth endpoints require a valid authenticated identity: an unauthenticated POST /resend-verification is rejected with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before any service work; the same authIdentityGuard protects GET /me.
testTitle: GET /me requires authentication and returns only the caller's public user

guarantee: Cross-organisation resource isolation: a user in org A cannot read or mutate an org-B resource through any auth endpoint.
gapDescription: The auth route group operates exclusively on the caller's own identity: getMe and resendEmailVerification key on request.user.userId (set by the guard from the verified session), while login/forgotPassword/resetPassword/verifyEmail key on a self-supplied email or an unguessable single-use sha256-hashed token. None of these endpoints take an org-scoped resource id from the client, so there is no sibling resource to leak across orgs. Cross-org guard behaviour (org derived from current DB row) is itself covered in auth-isolation.test.ts.

guarantee: Subscription/plan gating (subscriptionGuard, requireCompletePlan) on auth endpoints.
gapDescription: No auth endpoint registers subscriptionGuard or requireCompletePlan; /me and /resend-verification use only authIdentityGuard, and the remaining endpoints are public. register deliberately provisions a TRIALING ESSENTIALS subscription rather than gating on one. Plan-gating therefore does not apply to this route group.

guarantee: GET / returns ONLY the authenticated caller's organisation: the service calls organisation.findUnique with where.id === request.user.organisationId and never with any client-supplied id, so a user in org A can never read org B's organisation record.
testTitle: getOrganisation scopes the lookup to the caller's organisationId

guarantee: PATCH / writes ONLY to the caller's organisation: organisation.update is always called with where.id === request.user.organisationId, so a user in org A can never mutate org B's organisation record.
testTitle: updateOrganisation scopes the update to the caller's organisationId

guarantee: The GET/PATCH response body never leaks fields outside the publicOrganisation allow-list (id, name, rcnNumber, croNumber, legalForm, complexity, charitablePurpose, financialYearEnd, registeredAddress, contactEmail, contactPhone, website, dateRegistered, lastAgmDate) — i.e. no internal/cross-tenant columns are returned.
testTitle: GET / returns only the public organisation field allow-list

guarantee: PATCH / rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls organisation.update.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot PATCH the organisation (requireAdmin)

guarantee: PATCH / is permitted for ADMIN and OWNER (requireAdmin = requireRole('OWNER','ADMIN')), reaching the service and returning 200.
testTitle: an ADMIN may PATCH the organisation

guarantee: GET / does NOT require admin: a MEMBER with an active subscription can read the organisation (read is gated only by authGuard+subscriptionGuard, not requireAdmin).
testTitle: a MEMBER can read the organisation

guarantee: Both GET / and PATCH / are blocked by subscriptionGuard with 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} when the organisation has no subscription record, and the service/update is never reached.
testTitle: organisation routes require an active subscription

guarantee: An expired-trial / past-due-grace-expired / inactive subscription blocks GET and PATCH with the corresponding 403 code (TRIAL_EXPIRED | PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED | SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE).
testTitle: organisation routes reject an expired trial

guarantee: PATCH / rejects malformed bodies with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (e.g. name longer than 300 chars, contactEmail not an email, website not a URL, dateInputSchema rejecting non-ISO dates, legalForm/complexity/charitablePurpose outside their enums) and never calls organisation.update or leaks a 500/stack.
testTitle: PATCH / rejects malformed bodies with VALIDATION_ERROR and skips the write

guarantee: Both endpoints reject unauthenticated requests (missing/invalid/revoked token) with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and reject authenticated-but-email-unverified users with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} via authGuard, before any service call.
testTitle: organisation routes require authentication

guarantee: PUT /records/:standardId with a MEMBER token returns 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} and the complianceRecord.upsert mock is NOT called.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot upsert a compliance record (requireAdmin)

guarantee: PUT /signoff with a MEMBER token returns 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} and the complianceSignoff.upsert mock is NOT called.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot update the board sign-off (requireAdmin)

guarantee: An ADMIN (and OWNER) token is accepted by requireAdmin on both PUT /records/:standardId and PUT /signoff (reaches the service and returns 2xx).
testTitle: an ADMIN may upsert a compliance record and the board sign-off

guarantee: Every compliance endpoint with a missing/invalid Bearer token returns 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and no service query runs.
testTitle: compliance routes require authentication

guarantee: When the organisation has no subscription row, every scope-resolving compliance read/write (getRecords/getRecord/getSummary/getPrinciplesForOrganisation/upsertRecord) throws AppError 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before any compliance table is touched.
testTitle: compliance writes are refused (and not persisted) without a subscription

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS/SIMPLE org's compliance reads (records, summary, principles) are restricted to isCore standards; a COMPLETE/COMPLEX org receives additional standards.
testTitle: getRecords for an Essentials/Simple org restricts to core standards

guarantee: upsertRecord for an out-of-plan (non-core) standard on an ESSENTIALS org throws 403 {code:'COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN'} and does NOT write the record; an unknown standard throws 404 STANDARD_NOT_FOUND; a core standard or a COMPLETE/COMPLEX additional standard is allowed.
testTitle: upsertRecord blocks a non-core standard for an Essentials org (no billing bypass)

guarantee: getRecord for a non-core standard on an ESSENTIALS org throws 403 COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN (read path is gated identically to the write path via ensureStandardIncludedInPlan).
testTitle: getRecord refuses an out-of-plan standard for an Essentials org

guarantee: upsertRecord writes only to the requesting org: the complianceRecord.upsert where-key and create payload carry organisationId from request.user, so a record for standard s1 in org A can never be created/overwritten under org B.
testTitle: upsertRecord scopes the composite key and create payload to the caller organisation

guarantee: getRecord/getRecords/getSummary never return another org's compliance records: each prisma query embeds organisationId from request.user (composite key for getRecord, where.organisationId for getRecords/getSummary).
testTitle: compliance reads are scoped to the caller organisation

guarantee: upsertSignoff and getSignoff operate only on the caller org's sign-off: the complianceSignoff composite key organisationId_reportingYear is built from request.user.organisationId, so org B can never read or overwrite org A's board approval.
testTitle: upsertSignoff scopes the composite key to the caller organisation

guarantee: PUT /records/:standardId rejects a malformed body (e.g. reportingYear out of [2018,2100], a non-enum status, or an over-5000-char text field) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and does NOT call complianceRecord.upsert.
testTitle: PUT /records rejects an invalid body before writing

guarantee: PUT /signoff enforces the superRefine rule: status 'APPROVED' without boardMeetingDate/minuteReference/approvedByName returns 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and does NOT call complianceSignoff.upsert; an invalid status enum or a non-ISO boardMeetingDate is also 400.
testTitle: PUT /signoff requires approval evidence when status is APPROVED

guarantee: GET endpoints that require ?year reject a missing or non-numeric/out-of-range year with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (complianceQuerySchema), not a 500.
testTitle: compliance year-scoped reads reject a missing or invalid year

guarantee: GET /principles/:principleId returns 404 {code:'PRINCIPLE_NOT_FOUND'} for an unknown principle id (rather than 500 or leaking null).
testTitle: GET /principles/:id returns 404 for an unknown principle

guarantee: subscriptionGuard blocks compliance routes for trial-expired / past-due-grace-expired / inactive subscriptions with the matching 403 code (TRIAL_EXPIRED | PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED | SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE | NO_SUBSCRIPTION).
testTitle: compliance routes are blocked when the subscription is not in good standing

guarantee: Not applicable: the compliance route group calls no external provider (Stripe/Supabase/Resend) — it only reads/writes Prisma — so there is no provider-unconfigured 503 path to prove.
gapDescription: ComplianceService and the route handlers depend solely on app.prisma; no Stripe/Supabase/Resend client is constructed or invoked anywhere in routes/compliance/index.ts or services/compliance.service.ts, so the graceful-degradation concern does not apply to this area.

guarantee: Not applicable: compliance writes are upserts keyed on (organisationId, standardId, reportingYear) for records and (organisationId, reportingYear) for sign-off, which are naturally idempotent; there are no webhooks, reminder dedupe logs, or reconciliation retries in this route group.
gapDescription: The idempotency concern targets StripeWebhookEvent / DeadlineReminderLog / document-deletion reconciliation, none of which exist in the compliance area. The upsert-by-composite-key design means repeated PUTs are inherently safe, so there is no separate dedupe mechanism to prove.

guarantee: BoardMemberService.update(orgA, id-of-org-B-member, ...) throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls prisma.boardMember.update (no cross-org write).
testTitle: board member update rejects a member from another organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.remove(orgA, id-of-org-B-member) throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls prisma.boardMember.delete (no cross-org delete).
testTitle: board member remove rejects a member from another organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.create stamps the caller's organisationId onto the new record (a caller cannot create a board member attributed to another org).
testTitle: board member create scopes to the organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.list(orgA, ...) passes where {organisationId: orgA} to both findMany and count, so it can only return org A's board members and its total reflects only org A.
testTitle: board member list scopes findMany and count to the organisation

guarantee: A MEMBER token is rejected with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} on POST /, PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id (requireAdmin blocks writes), while an ADMIN is allowed (POST / returns 201).
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot create, update, or delete board members (requireAdmin)

guarantee: GET / (list) is readable by a non-admin MEMBER (read is intentionally not requireAdmin-gated) and returns 200.
testTitle: a MEMBER can still read board members, and an ADMIN can create them

guarantee: Every board-member endpoint rejects an unauthenticated request with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} (authGuard onRequest hook applies to the whole group).
testTitle: board-member write routes reject unauthenticated requests

guarantee: Board-member endpoints require an active subscription: subscriptionGuard rejects a missing subscription with 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'}.
testTitle: board-member routes reject an expired trial and inactive subscription

guarantee: POST / with a malformed body (e.g. missing required name/role/appointedDate, or appointedDate not an ISO date) is rejected with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.boardMember.create (no partial record written, no 500/stack leak).
testTitle: POST /board-members rejects malformed bodies with VALIDATION_ERROR and does not create

guarantee: PATCH /:id with a malformed body (e.g. email not an email, or appointedDate not ISO) is rejected with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.boardMember.update or findFirst (no foreign lookup or write on invalid input).
testTitle: PATCH /board-members/:id rejects an invalid email with VALIDATION_ERROR and does not update

guarantee: Board-member endpoints do not integrate with Stripe/Supabase/Resend (the service is constructed with only prisma), so there is no external-dependency 503 path to prove for this route group.
gapDescription: Non-obvious because sibling route groups (team, billing, documents) take email/storage/Stripe clients; board-members deliberately does not, so the graceful-degradation concern has no applicable surface here.

guarantee: GET /:id for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user, because DocumentService.getById scopes findFirst by {id, organisationId} and throws AppError 404 when null.
testTitle: GET /:id returns 404 for a document belonging to another organisation

guarantee: GET /:id/download for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user (service.getStoragePath scopes findFirst by {id, organisationId}), and never calls StorageService.getSignedUrl.
testTitle: GET /:id/download returns 404 and never signs a URL for a foreign-org document

guarantee: StorageService.getSignedUrl rejects a storagePath outside the caller's organisation prefix (org-b/..., ../org-a/..., bare org id) with AppError 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} before any Supabase access, so a tampered fileUrl cannot sign another org's object.
testTitle: getSignedUrl rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before storage access

guarantee: StorageService.deleteFile rejects a storagePath outside the caller's organisation prefix with AppError 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} before any Supabase access, so the delete path cannot remove another org's object.
testTitle: deleteFile rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before storage access

guarantee: GET /_local-download enforces the organisation prefix on the supplied ?path via StorageService.readLocalFile -> assertOrganisationStoragePath, returning 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} when an org-A user requests an org-B path, preventing cross-org local-file reads.
testTitle: readLocalFile rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before reading

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard (and alias /:id/standards) for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user and never calls documentStandardLink.create, because linkStandard scopes the document findFirst by {id, organisationId}.
testTitle: POST /:id/standards rejects linking when the document belongs to another organisation

guarantee: DELETE /:id/unlink-standard (and alias /:id/standards/:standardId) for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user and never calls documentStandardLink.deleteMany, because unlinkStandard scopes the document findFirst by {id, organisationId}.
testTitle: DELETE /:id/standards/:standardId rejects unlinking when the document belongs to another organisation

guarantee: DELETE /:id for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user, never creates a DocumentStorageDeletion outbox row, never deletes the DB row, and never calls StorageService.deleteFile (remove() scopes the findFirst by {id, organisationId}).
testTitle: DELETE /:id rejects and performs no side effects for a document belonging to another organisation

guarantee: Uploaded objects are namespaced to the caller's organisation: StorageService.uploadFile writes to `${organisationId}/<timestamp>-<sanitised>` so one org's upload can never overwrite or land under another org's prefix.
testTitle: document upload stores the object under the caller's organisation prefix

guarantee: POST / (upload) rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls StorageService.uploadFile or document.create.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot upload a document (requireAdmin)

guarantee: DELETE /:id rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls service.remove or StorageService.deleteFile.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot delete a document (requireAdmin)

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard, POST /:id/standards, DELETE /:id/unlink-standard, and DELETE /:id/standards/:standardId each reject a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never call documentStandardLink.create/deleteMany.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot link or unlink a document to a governance standard (requireAdmin)

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS (or COMPLEX-but-not-COMPLETE / SIMPLE) organisation cannot link a document to a non-core ('additional') governance standard: linkStandard throws AppError 403 {code:'COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN'} and never calls documentStandardLink.create.
testTitle: Essentials organisations cannot link documents to additional governance standards

guarantee: Uploads are gated by a per-plan storage quota (ESSENTIALS 2 GiB, COMPLETE 10 GiB): when used+requested bytes exceed the plan quota, create throws AppError 403 {code:'DOCUMENT_STORAGE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED'} before document.create runs, and the already-uploaded object is cleaned up.
testTitle: document upload rejects files that would exceed the plan storage quota and cleans up storage

guarantee: Upload with no subscription record is blocked: assertStorageQuota throws AppError 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before document.create, so an org without a subscription cannot store documents.
testTitle: document upload is blocked when the organisation has no subscription record

guarantee: subscriptionGuard blocks every documents endpoint when the subscription is expired/inactive: a TRIAL_EXPIRED / PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED / SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE org receives 403 with the corresponding code on GET / and POST /, regardless of role.
testTitle: documents endpoints are blocked when the subscription trial has expired

guarantee: Upload rejects a file whose declared MIME type is allowed but whose magic-byte signature does not match (e.g. a .pdf that is not %PDF-), returning 400 {code:'INVALID_FILE_SIGNATURE'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects files whose signature does not match the claimed document type

guarantee: Upload rejects a disallowed MIME type (e.g. application/msword legacy macro-capable Office) with 400 {code:'INVALID_MIME_TYPE'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects macro-capable legacy Office files before storage

guarantee: Upload of a file exceeding the 10 MB limit returns 413 {code:'FILE_TOO_LARGE'} (translating the Fastify FST_REQ_FILE_TOO_LARGE / multipart fileSize error) without storing the file.
testTitle: document upload translates multipart file size errors to 413

guarantee: Upload with too many multipart fields, more than one file, or an oversized field value returns 413 {code:'MULTIPART_LIMIT_EXCEEDED'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects requests with too many multipart fields before storage

guarantee: Upload with malformed metadata fields (missing required name, invalid category enum, oversized name/description/owner, malformed approvedDate/nextReviewDate) is rejected by uploadDocumentSchema with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and a details array, never a 500 or stack leak.
testTitle: document upload rejects malformed metadata with VALIDATION_ERROR before storage

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard (and aliases) reject a missing/empty standardId via linkStandardSchema with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} before any documentStandardLink write.
testTitle: POST /:id/standards rejects a missing standardId with VALIDATION_ERROR

guarantee: GET /_local-download without a ?path query returns 400 {code:'LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH_REQUIRED'} rather than a 500.
testTitle: GET /_local-download requires a path query parameter

guarantee: When the database create fails after a successful storage upload, the route deletes the just-stored object (StorageService.deleteFile called with the uploaded org/path) and surfaces the original error (500), preventing orphaned storage objects.
testTitle: document upload deletes the stored object when database creation fails

guarantee: When the database create fails AND the compensating storage cleanup also fails, the route preserves and returns the original database error (e.g. 400 DOCUMENT_CREATE_FAILED) rather than masking it with the cleanup error.
testTitle: document upload preserves the database error when storage cleanup fails

guarantee: When Supabase storage is unconfigured (no SUPABASE_URL / SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and not the local driver), the download path (GET /:id/download -> StorageService.getSignedUrl -> getSupabaseClient) returns a clean 503 {code:'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'} rather than a 500/stack leak.
testTitle: GET /:id/download returns 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED when Supabase is unconfigured

guarantee: GET /_local-download returns 503 {code:'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'} when the local storage driver is not enabled (readLocalFile guards isLocalStorageDriver), so the endpoint degrades cleanly instead of attempting a filesystem read.
testTitle: GET /_local-download returns 503 when the local storage driver is disabled

guarantee: Document deletion is crash-safe and reconciles exactly once: remove() creates a DocumentStorageDeletion outbox row and deletes the DB row in one transaction (outbox before storage), then deletes storage and marks the outbox row processed; ordering is outbox-create -> db-delete -> storage-delete -> mark-processed.
testTitle: document delete removes storage after deleting the database record

guarantee: If the DB delete fails inside remove()'s transaction, no storage object is deleted and the outbox row is not processed (the whole delete rolls back), so storage is never removed without a committed DB delete.
testTitle: document delete does not remove storage when database deletion fails

guarantee: If post-commit storage cleanup fails, the request still succeeds (204) and the failure is recorded on the outbox row (attempts incremented, sanitized lastError, claimedAt cleared) so the retry job can re-process it safely.
testTitle: document delete reports success when post-delete storage cleanup fails

guarantee: retryPendingStorageDeletions reconciles pending outbox rows exactly once: it claims unprocessed rows (via FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED when Postgres is available, else findMany of processedAt:null), deletes storage, marks processed on success, and on failure increments attempts + records a sanitized lastError + clears claimedAt (leaving the row pending for a safe retry).
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes pending cleanup records

guarantee: A document-storage-cleanup job failure (the retry call throwing) fires the error-alert webhook with method JOB, url /jobs/document-storage-cleanup, code DOCUMENT_STORAGE_CLEANUP_FAILED, and no raw secrets/emails/paths in the payload or log.
testTitle: runDocumentStorageCleanup sends a sanitized operational alert when storage cleanup fails

guarantee: When the cleanup run completes without throwing but some storage deletions failed (result.failed > 0), the job still fires the error-alert webhook (errorName DocumentStorageCleanupFailure) so silent partial failures are surfaced.
testTitle: runDocumentStorageCleanup alerts when cleanup records failed storage deletions without throwing

guarantee: The readiness endpoint reflects real document-storage health: storageConfigured (StorageService.isConfigured) and storageBucketReachable (verifyBucket, with a readiness timeout) gate readiness, and a public/unreachable bucket flips readiness to 503 not_ready.
testTitle: readiness reports not_ready when the document storage bucket is unreachable

guarantee: DeadlineService.update(orgA, idOwnedByOrgB, data) performs findFirst({where:{id, organisationId:orgA}}), gets null, throws AppError 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND, and never calls prisma.deadline.update.
testTitle: update rejects a deadline from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: DeadlineService.remove(orgA, idOwnedByOrgB) performs findFirst({where:{id, organisationId:orgA}}), gets null, throws AppError 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND, and never calls prisma.deadline.delete.
testTitle: remove rejects a deadline from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: DeadlineService.list(orgId, ...) issues findMany and count whose where clause is exactly {organisationId: orgId}, so a caller can only ever read deadlines of their own organisation.
testTitle: list reports hasMore based on total versus the returned page

guarantee: DeadlineService.create(orgId, data) sets data.organisationId to the caller's orgId (a member cannot create a deadline owned by another organisation).
testTitle: create scopes to the organisation and defaults reminderDays

guarantee: End-to-end through the HTTP stack: PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id for a deadline owned by org B, called with a valid org-A admin Bearer token, return 404 {code:'DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND'} and never invoke prisma.deadline.update / prisma.deadline.delete.
testTitle: PATCH/DELETE /:id for a deadline owned by another organisation returns 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND without writing

guarantee: A MEMBER (valid token, active subscription) is rejected with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} on POST /, PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id (requireAdmin), and the create/update/delete prisma write mocks are never called.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot create, update, or delete deadlines (requireAdmin)

guarantee: An ADMIN/OWNER (valid token, active subscription) is allowed through requireAdmin: POST / returns 201, PATCH /:id returns 200, DELETE /:id returns 204.
testTitle: an ADMIN may create, update, and delete deadlines

guarantee: GET / (list) is readable by a MEMBER (it carries authGuard+subscriptionGuard but NOT requireAdmin), returning 200.
testTitle: a MEMBER can read the deadline list

guarantee: Every deadline route (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE) rejects a request with no/invalid Bearer token, or a revoked session, with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before the handler runs.
testTitle: deadline routes require authentication

guarantee: A valid token whose owning user has emailVerified:false is rejected with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} on deadline routes.
testTitle: deadline routes reject a user whose email is not verified

guarantee: All deadline routes are blocked by subscriptionGuard when the organisation has no subscription (403 NO_SUBSCRIPTION), an expired trial (403 TRIAL_EXPIRED), a past-due grace-expired sub (403 PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED), or an inactive sub (403 SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE), before the handler runs.
testTitle: deadline routes require an active subscription

guarantee: COMPLETE-only (requireCompletePlan) gating does not apply to the deadlines route group.
gapDescription: routes/deadlines/index.ts wires only authGuard, subscriptionGuard and requireAdmin; it never imports or applies requireCompletePlan, so there is no COMPLETE-plan feature boundary to prove for this group (deadlines are available on every active plan).

guarantee: POST / rejects a malformed/oversized create body (missing title, title>300 chars, missing/invalid dueDate, description>1000 chars, reminderDays element out of 1..365 or non-integer) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.deadline.create.
testTitle: POST / rejects malformed create bodies with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and does not write

guarantee: PATCH /:id rejects a malformed update body (e.g. title>300, invalid dueDate string, isComplete non-boolean, reminderDays out of range) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.deadline.update.
testTitle: PATCH /:id rejects malformed update bodies with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and does not write

guarantee: A Stripe webhook event whose id already exists in StripeWebhookEvent is ignored before any Stripe retrieve or subscription mutation: handleWebhook returns early and neither stripe.subscriptions.retrieve, stripeWebhookEvent.create, nor subscription.upsert is called.
testTitle: duplicate Stripe webhook event ids are ignored before subscription mutation

guarantee: When two concurrent deliveries of the same event race past the pre-check, the in-transaction stripeWebhookEvent.create P2002 unique violation causes the second to return without mutating the subscription, while a P2002 originating from a different write (e.g. subscription.upsert on stripeSubscriptionId) is rethrown rather than swallowed.
testTitle: Stripe webhook unique errors outside the event ledger are rethrown

guarantee: The webhook ledger row is created inside the same $transaction as the subscription mutation (the create-then-mutate is atomic), so a failed subscription write does not leave a ledger row that would suppress a legitimate retry.
testTitle: a failed subscription write rolls back the webhook ledger row so the event can be retried

guarantee: POST /billing/webhooks with an invalid stripe-signature header returns 400 {code:'INVALID_STRIPE_SIGNATURE'} and never reaches handleWebhook (no subscription write).
testTitle: invalid Stripe webhook signatures return a client error

guarantee: POST /billing/webhooks with no stripe-signature header returns 400 {code:'MISSING_STRIPE_SIGNATURE'} and never reaches handleWebhook.
testTitle: missing Stripe webhook signatures return a client error

guarantee: createCheckoutSchema accepts only plan in {ESSENTIALS,COMPLETE} and interval in {monthly,yearly}; an unsupported interval/plan is rejected by the schema.
testTitle: POST /checkout with an invalid plan returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls Stripe

guarantee: A webhook event whose checkout metadata is missing/invalid (no organisationId, unrecognised plan, or no subscription id) is rejected with 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH and performs no subscription write.
testTitle: checkout.session.completed rejects metadata when the customer or price does not match the organisation plan

guarantee: POST /billing/checkout (and /create-checkout) reject a MEMBER and an ADMIN with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireOwner, and the BillingService createCheckoutSession (organisation.findUniqueOrThrow / Stripe) is never invoked.
testTitle: checkout and portal require OWNER (MEMBER and ADMIN are rejected with FORBIDDEN)

guarantee: POST /billing/portal (and /create-portal) reject a MEMBER and an ADMIN with 403 FORBIDDEN via requireOwner before createPortalSession runs.
testTitle: checkout and portal require OWNER (MEMBER and ADMIN are rejected with FORBIDDEN)

guarantee: All authed billing routes (/checkout, /create-checkout, /portal, /create-portal, /status) reject an unauthenticated request with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} via the authGuard onRequest hook, before any service call.
testTitle: authed billing routes reject unauthenticated requests with 401

guarantee: GET /billing/status returns the subscription for the caller's own organisationId only: getStatus queries subscription.findUnique({where:{organisationId: request.user.organisationId}}), so an OWNER of org A can never observe org B's plan/status/access.
testTitle: GET /status scopes the subscription lookup to the caller's organisation

guarantee: A checkout.session.completed webhook can only attach a subscription to the organisation that owns the matching stripeCustomerId: if the session/subscription customer id does not equal organisation.stripeCustomerId, the handler throws 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH and performs no subscription.upsert (preventing an attacker-controlled session from binding a subscription to a foreign org).
testTitle: checkout.session.completed rejects metadata when the customer or price does not match the organisation plan

guarantee: customer.subscription.updated and .deleted only mutate the local subscription whose stripeSubscriptionId matches and whose organisation.stripeCustomerId matches the event's customer; a mismatched customer id throws 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH (via verifyExistingSubscription) and an unknown stripeSubscriptionId returns without writing.
testTitle: subscription.updated and .deleted reject a customer that does not own the local subscription and ignore unknown subscription ids

guarantee: When STRIPE_SECRET_KEY/STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET/price ids are unconfigured, checkout, portal and the webhook signature step fail with a clean AppError 503 {code:'BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED'} (handled to a 503 response) rather than a 500 or stack leak.
testTitle: checkout returns 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED when Stripe is unconfigured

guarantee: GET /billing/status degrades softly when billing is unconfigured: it returns 200 with billingConfigured:false (and hasAccess derived from stored subscription) instead of a 503, so the status endpoint keeps working when Stripe env is absent.
testTitle: billing status reports billingConfigured:false without erroring when Stripe is unconfigured

guarantee: BillingService.isConfigured() truthfully reflects Stripe readiness (all of secret key, webhook secret, and every plan price id configured) and feeds the readiness endpoint's billingConfigured check, so readiness reports not_ready when billing secrets are missing.
testTitle: isConfigured returns false when any required Stripe price id is missing

guarantee: plan-gating (requireCompletePlan / subscriptionGuard blocking expired tenants) does not apply to the billing route group itself.
gapDescription: The billing endpoints are intentionally NOT behind subscriptionGuard or requireCompletePlan: checkout/portal/status must remain reachable precisely when a subscription is absent, expired, or past-due so an owner can pay or fix billing. Gating them would deadlock recovery. (The subscriptionGuard/grace-window tests in this file prove gating for OTHER route groups, not billing.) Webhook->plan correctness is covered separately by the checkout price/metadata mismatch test.

guarantee: auth-session rotation/replay does not apply to the billing group; the webhook route is unauthenticated (signature-verified) and the authed routes rely on the shared authGuard with no billing-specific token handling, and the service logs neither raw bodies, signatures, nor secrets.
gapDescription: Refresh-token rotation/replay is owned by the auth route group, not billing. The only billing logging is app.log.warn({code,statusCode}) / app.log.error(err) in the webhook catch (index.ts lines 36-39), which logs the AppError code and status but not the raw body or stripe-signature, so there is no billing-specific secret-logging guarantee to prove here.

guarantee: GET /compliance-record for an org whose subscription.plan is ESSENTIALS (or anything other than COMPLETE) never invokes loadGovernanceRegisters: none of conflictRecord/riskRecord/complaintRecord/fundraisingRecord/annualReportReadiness/financialControlReview findMany/findUnique are called, and the response body contains no 'Governance registers' heading nor any register data.
testTitle: Essentials exports do not include Complete-only governance registers

guarantee: GET /compliance-record for an org whose subscription.plan is COMPLETE DOES read and embed the governance registers: loadGovernanceRegisters runs and the response body includes the 'Governance registers' heading plus the org's conflict/risk/complaint/fundraising rows.
testTitle: Complete plan exports include governance registers

guarantee: An org with no active subscription cannot reach the export at all: subscriptionGuard returns 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before the handler runs, and organisation.findUniqueOrThrow / the compliance reads are never executed.
testTitle: export rejects organisation with no subscription

guarantee: An org whose trial has expired (status TRIALING, trialEndsAt in the past) is blocked from the export with 403 {code:'TRIAL_EXPIRED'} and the report HTML is never built.
testTitle: export rejects organisation with expired trial

guarantee: The report is always built for request.user.organisationId only: organisation.findUniqueOrThrow is called with where{id: request.user.organisationId} and the compliance principle/record/signoff/subscription reads are scoped to that same id, so a caller authenticated as org A can never render org B's organisation header, RCN, principles, records or signoff.
testTitle: export scopes the organisation and compliance reads to the token organisation

guarantee: On the COMPLETE plan, every governance-register read in loadGovernanceRegisters is scoped to request.user.organisationId: conflictRecord/riskRecord/complaintRecord/fundraisingRecord findMany are called with where{organisationId}, and annualReportReadiness/financialControlReview findUnique with where{organisationId_reportingYear:{organisationId, reportingYear}}, so a COMPLETE org A export can never embed org B's registers.
testTitle: Complete plan governance registers are scoped to the token organisation

guarantee: A request with no/invalid/expired token, a revoked session, or a missing user is rejected by authGuard with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before the export handler runs; an authenticated but email-unverified user is rejected with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'}.
testTitle: export rejects missing token, revoked session, and unverified email

guarantee: GET /compliance-record with a malformed, missing, out-of-range, or oversized year (e.g. year=abc, year omitted, year=1999, year=99999) returns 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (never a 500 or stack leak) and the report HTML / organisation lookup is never reached for the invalid value.
testTitle: export rejects malformed year with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR

guarantee: User-supplied compliance/register text (e.g. standard titles, actionTaken, evidence, explanationIfNA, trusteeName, matter, mitigation, complaint summary, controls) is HTML-escaped via escapeHtml/simpleTable before being embedded in the exported report, so a stored '<script>' or '"' in a record cannot break out of its cell or inject markup into the generated HTML.
testTitle: export HTML-escapes stored record and register values

guarantee: The export does not depend on Stripe/Supabase/Resend at request time (no checkout, storage, or email call), so it stays available when those externals are down; conversely if the org row is missing, organisation.findUniqueOrThrow surfaces a clean error via handleError (404/500) rather than leaking a stack.
gapDescription: The export handler makes only Prisma reads — it never calls Stripe, Supabase storage, or Resend — so the 503-on-external-outage concern has no surface here. The only error path is Prisma findUniqueOrThrow / unexpected errors, which handleError maps to a safe INTERNAL_ERROR 500 without exposing the message in production (covered conceptually by errors.ts behaviour). No export-specific external-degradation test is warranted.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ with no Authorization header (or an invalid/expired token) returns 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and never reaches the aggregate reads; a revoked session token is likewise rejected 401.
testTitle: dashboard route rejects unauthenticated requests

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ for an authenticated user whose email is unverified returns 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} and performs none of the aggregate reads.
testTitle: dashboard route rejects users with an unverified email

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ for an organisation with no subscription returns 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'}; an expired trial returns 403 {code:'TRIAL_EXPIRED'}; an ended past-due grace returns 403 {code:'PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED'}; an otherwise-inactive subscription returns 403 {code:'SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE'} — in every case before any aggregate read runs.
testTitle: dashboard route requires an active subscription

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ scopes the inline upcoming-deadlines read to the caller's org: deadline.findMany is invoked with where.organisationId === request.user.organisationId (plus isComplete:false and dueDate.gte), so deadlines of another org can never appear in upcomingDeadlines.
testTitle: dashboard scopes upcoming deadlines to the requesting organisation

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ scopes the inline board-members read (which drives boardAlerts) to the caller's org: boardMember.findMany is invoked with where.organisationId === request.user.organisationId, so no foreign-org member can surface as a conduct_unsigned / induction_pending / term_expiring alert.
testTitle: dashboard scopes board-alert source members to the requesting organisation

guarantee: The compliance summary embedded in the dashboard is org-scoped: ComplianceService.getSummary filters complianceRecord.findMany by where.organisationId === the caller's org and resolves the org's complexity/plan via organisation.findUniqueOrThrow({id:organisationId}) and subscription.findUnique({organisationId}), so another org's records never affect the dashboard compliance figures.
testTitle: getSummary scopes the compliance-record query to the requested organisation

guarantee: Every per-resource query inside ActivityService.getRecentActivity is scoped to the caller's org: complianceRecord.findMany, document.findMany, boardMember.findMany and deadline.findMany are each invoked with where.organisationId === the org argument, and the org's plan/complexity are resolved via organisation.findUniqueOrThrow({id:organisationId}) / subscription.findUnique({organisationId}); so no foreign-org compliance update, document upload, board-member change or deadline change can appear in recentActivity.
testTitle: recent activity scopes every source query to the requested organisation

guarantee: ActivityService.getRecentActivity includes additional (non-core) compliance standards in the feed only when the org is COMPLEX AND on the COMPLETE plan; otherwise it scopes complianceRecord.findMany to standard.isCore:true, so an ESSENTIALS org never sees additional-standard compliance activity on its dashboard.
testTitle: Essentials recent activity excludes additional-standard compliance records

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ surfaces an internal failure (e.g. a rejected prisma read) as a controlled error response via handleError rather than leaking a stack trace or returning an unhandled 500 with internals.
testTitle: dashboard route returns a clean error when an aggregate read fails

guarantee: Concern does not apply: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ accepts no request body and no validated path or query parameters (organisationId and year are derived server-side from request.user and new Date()), so there is no client-supplied input to validate or reject.

Concern does not apply: the dashboard group has a single read-only GET endpoint and uses no requireAdmin/requireOwner guard. Any authenticated, subscribed member of an org may read their own org's dashboard; there is no role-restricted write or owner-only operation to enforce.
updateConflict for a conflictRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 CONFLICT_NOT_FOUND and never calls conflictRecord.update.
testTitle: updateConflict rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: removeConflict for a conflictRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 CONFLICT_NOT_FOUND and never calls conflictRecord.delete.
testTitle: removeConflict rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: updateRisk for a riskRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 RISK_NOT_FOUND and never calls riskRecord.update.
testTitle: updateRisk rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: removeRisk for a riskRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 RISK_NOT_FOUND and never calls riskRecord.delete.
testTitle: removeRisk rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: updateComplaint for a complaintRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 COMPLAINT_NOT_FOUND and never calls complaintRecord.update.
testTitle: updateComplaint rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: removeComplaint for a complaintRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 COMPLAINT_NOT_FOUND and never calls complaintRecord.delete.
testTitle: removeComplaint rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: updateFundraising for a fundraisingRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 FUNDRAISING_NOT_FOUND and never calls fundraisingRecord.update.
testTitle: updateFundraising rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: removeFundraising for a fundraisingRecord owned by org B, called with org A's organisationId, throws AppError 404 FUNDRAISING_NOT_FOUND and never calls fundraisingRecord.delete.
testTitle: removeFundraising rejects a record from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: The org-scoped record lookup keys on BOTH id and organisationId (findFirst where {id, organisationId}), so an id alone cannot select another org's record.
testTitle: the org-scoped lookup keys on both id and organisationId

guarantee: createConflict with a boardMemberId belonging to another org throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND (lookup scoped {id, organisationId}) and never calls conflictRecord.create.
testTitle: conflict records reject board members from another organisation on create

guarantee: updateConflict that re-assigns boardMemberId to another org's board member throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls conflictRecord.update.
testTitle: conflict records reject board members from another organisation on update

guarantee: listConflicts/listRisks/listComplaints/listFundraising each issue findMany with where {organisationId} so a caller only ever sees their own org's records (no global list leak).
testTitle: list register methods scope findMany to the caller organisation

guarantee: summary() counts conflicts/risks/complaints/fundraising with where {organisationId, status:{not:'CLOSED'}}, so the dashboard totals never include another org's records.
testTitle: summary counts only non-closed records scoped to the organisation

guarantee: getAnnualReportReadiness / upsertAnnualReportReadiness only ever address rows via the compound unique organisationId_reportingYear, so org A can never read or overwrite org B's annual-report readiness row.
testTitle: annual report readiness reads and upserts are scoped to organisationId_reportingYear

guarantee: getFinancialControlReview / upsertFinancialControlReview only ever address rows via the compound unique organisationId_reportingYear, so org A can never read or overwrite org B's financial-controls row.
testTitle: financial control review reads and upserts are scoped to organisationId_reportingYear

guarantee: POST /conflicts by a MEMBER is rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin and conflictRecord.create is never called.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot create conflict records (requireAdmin)

guarantee: PATCH /conflicts/:id and DELETE /conflicts/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without reaching conflictRecord.update/delete.

guarantee: POST /risks, PATCH /risks/:id, DELETE /risks/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any riskRecord write.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot write risk records (requireAdmin)

guarantee: POST /complaints, PATCH /complaints/:id, DELETE /complaints/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any complaintRecord write.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot write complaint records (requireAdmin)

guarantee: POST /fundraising, PATCH /fundraising/:id, DELETE /fundraising/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any fundraisingRecord write.
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot write fundraising records (requireAdmin)

guarantee: PUT /annual-report and PUT /financial-controls by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without reaching annualReportReadiness.upsert / financialControlReview.upsert.

guarantee: An ADMIN (the minimum allowed write role) is permitted through requireAdmin on a register write (e.g. POST /risks returns 201), confirming the guard is not over-restrictive.
testTitle: an ADMIN may create register records (requireAdmin allows ADMIN)

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS-plan org calling GET /summary is blocked 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE by requireCompletePlan and the summary read (conflictRecord.count) is never executed.

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS-plan org is blocked 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE on the register READ endpoints other than summary (GET /conflicts, /risks, /complaints, /fundraising, /annual-report, /financial-controls), with no findMany/findUnique reaching the service.
testTitle: Essentials org is blocked from every governance register read endpoint

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS-plan org is blocked 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE on every register WRITE endpoint (POST/PATCH/DELETE conflicts/risks/complaints/fundraising, PUT annual-report, PUT financial-controls), with no create/update/delete/upsert reaching the service.
testTitle: Essentials org is blocked from every governance register write endpoint

guarantee: POST /conflicts with a malformed body (missing required trusteeName/matter/nature/dateDeclared/actionTaken, or a non-ISO dateDeclared, or oversized text >max) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and conflictRecord.create is never called.

guarantee: POST /risks with likelihood/impact outside 1-5 (or a non-enum category) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and riskRecord.create is never called.
testTitle: POST /risks with out-of-range likelihood returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and no write

guarantee: GET /summary, GET /annual-report and GET /financial-controls with a non-numeric/out-of-range ?year reject with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (complianceQuerySchema) rather than a 500.
testTitle: GET /summary rejects a malformed year query with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR

guarantee: PUT /annual-report and PUT /financial-controls with reportingYear outside 2018-2100 (or wrong type) reject with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and the upsert is never called.
testTitle: PUT /annual-report rejects an out-of-range reportingYear and never upserts

guarantee: PUT /annual-report accepts a valid governance-workflow body (reportingYear + narrative/booleans/filingStatus + ISO boardApprovalDate) without a validation error.
testTitle: annual report readiness accepts the expected governance workflow fields

guarantee: Every governance-registers endpoint requires a valid access token: a request with no/invalid token is rejected 401 UNAUTHORIZED by authGuard before any service call.
testTitle: governance register routes require authentication

guarantee: Concern does not apply: this route group performs no Stripe/Supabase/Resend calls; all six registers read/write only Prisma models. There is no external dependency whose outage would require a 503 here.
gapDescription: No Stripe/Supabase/Resend usage in routes/governance-registers/index.ts or services/governance-register.service.ts; degradation/503 behaviour is owned by billing/storage/email route groups, not this one.

guarantee: PATCH /members/:id/role for a member whose user row belongs to another organisation returns 404 MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls user.update (findFirst is scoped by {id, organisationId}).
testTitle: updateMemberRole rejects a member from another organisation (cross-tenant guard)

guarantee: DELETE /invites/:id for an invite belonging to another organisation returns 404 INVITE_NOT_FOUND and never calls teamInvite.update (findFirst is scoped by {id, organisationId}).

guarantee: acceptInvite creates the new user under the organisationId taken from the invite row (not from any request-controlled field), so an invite cannot place a user in a different org than the one that issued it.

guarantee: GET / (team list) returns only members and invites of the caller's organisationId, i.e. user.findMany and teamInvite.findMany are both invoked with where.organisationId === request.user.organisationId.

guarantee: POST /invites from a MEMBER is rejected with 403 FORBIDDEN and never creates a teamInvite (ensureCanInvite).

guarantee: POST /invites requesting role ADMIN from an ADMIN actor is rejected with 403 FORBIDDEN and only the OWNER may create ADMIN invites; a MEMBER-role invite from an ADMIN succeeds.

guarantee: PATCH /members/:id/role from a non-OWNER (ADMIN or MEMBER) is rejected with 403 FORBIDDEN and never calls user.update (ensureOwner).

guarantee: PATCH /members/:id/role cannot promote anyone to OWNER (returns 400 OWNER_TRANSFER_UNSUPPORTED), cannot demote the existing OWNER (400 CANNOT_DEMOTE_OWNER), and cannot change the actor's own role (400 CANNOT_CHANGE_OWN_ROLE) — in each case without performing user.update.

guarantee: Auth-gated team endpoints (GET /, POST /invites, DELETE /invites/:id, PATCH /members/:id/role) are blocked with 403 (NO_SUBSCRIPTION / TRIAL_EXPIRED / PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED / SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE) when the organisation has no active/within-grace subscription (subscriptionGuard).

guarantee: POST /invites with a malformed body (invalid email, or role not in {ADMIN,MEMBER}) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls teamInvite.create.

guarantee: PATCH /members/:id/role with a malformed body (role not in {OWNER,ADMIN,MEMBER}) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls user.update.

guarantee: POST /accept-invite issues fresh session tokens and sets auth cookies (201) only after the invite is atomically consumed; if consumption loses the race (updateMany count 0) or the user-email uniqueness check races, no session is issued and the response is 400 INVALID_INVITE.

guarantee: Auth-gated team endpoints (GET /, POST /invites, PATCH /members/:id/role) reject unauthenticated requests with 401 UNAUTHORIZED, while POST /accept-invite is reachable without auth (never 401) and validates the token to 400 INVALID_INVITE.

guarantee: Re-inviting an email that already has an active (non-expired, non-revoked, non-accepted) invite or that already belongs to a user is a no-op: no new invite is created, no email is sent, and the same neutral 202 message is returned; a DB unique-constraint race (P2002) on the active-invite index is also flattened to the same accepted response.
testTitle: team invites do not disclose why an invite recipient is unavailable

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness with a valid x-charitypilot-readiness-key returns 200 {status:'ready'} and checks={database:true, billingConfigured:true, emailConfigured:true, storageConfigured:true, storageBucketReachable:true} ONLY when every one of the five dependency probes resolves truthy.

guarantee: When the database probe fails (prisma.$queryRaw throws or hangs past READINESS_DEPENDENCY_TIMEOUT_MS), readiness returns 503 {status:'not_ready', checks.database:false} rather than a 500 or a hang, and the error is logged without crashing the request.

guarantee: When the database probe REJECTS (prisma.$queryRaw throws synchronously/asynchronously, e.g. connection refused) rather than hanging, readiness still returns 503 with checks.database:false and never propagates the error as a 500/stack leak.

guarantee: When Stripe (billing), Resend (email), or Supabase (storage) are unconfigured/unreachable, readiness reports the corresponding check as false and returns 503 not_ready, while the public liveness probe GET /api/v1/health continues to return 200 (the app keeps serving).

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} and exposes none of the dependency check field names when no readiness key header is supplied (with READINESS_API_KEY configured).

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} when the supplied x-charitypilot-readiness-key header is present but does NOT match the configured READINESS_API_KEY (wrong-key rejection), without running or exposing the dependency probes.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} when READINESS_API_KEY is unset or a placeholder (isConfiguredSecret false) even if a header is supplied — readiness can never be reached on an instance with no configured key.

guarantee: The readiness key comparison is timing-safe (SHA-256 digest + crypto.timingSafeEqual), never a plain === string comparison, so the gate is not vulnerable to timing side channels.

guarantee: Neither the supplied nor the configured readiness key value is ever echoed into the response body or logged in cleartext; the 401 response carries only a generic error/code and dependency-probe failures are logged without the key.

guarantee: A malformed/duplicated x-charitypilot-readiness-key header (e.g. sent as an array of values, which Fastify exposes as a non-string) is rejected with 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} rather than throwing a 500, because hasReadinessAccess requires typeof suppliedKey === 'string' before the timing-safe compare.
testTitle: readiness rejects a duplicated readiness key header without a 500

guarantee: Production env validation rejects a missing or too-short READINESS_API_KEY (must be >=32 chars and configured) so a weak/absent readiness gate can never reach production.

guarantee: N/A — neither health endpoint reads or mutates any organisation-scoped resource; the readiness probe only runs an unparameterised SELECT 1 and config/reachability checks, so there is no organisationId surface and no cross-org leak is possible.
gapDescription: No request.user, no organisationId, and no Prisma model query that returns tenant data — the only DB call is prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT 1`. There is nothing for an org-A user to read of org B here.

guarantee: N/A — health and readiness are infrastructure probes deliberately outside the subscription/plan model; they carry no requireCompletePlan or subscriptionGuard and must remain reachable regardless of any tenant's plan state.
gapDescription: The routes are registered with no auth/subscription/plan guards (server.ts line 81 registers healthRoutes with only a prefix). Gating readiness behind a subscription would defeat its purpose, so plan-gating does not apply.

guarantee: N/A — both endpoints are side-effect-free GET probes (no writes, no webhook ingestion, no reminder/dedupe logic), so there is no operation that must be processed exactly once.
gapDescription: Readiness only reads (SELECT 1, getBucket, config flags). StorageService.verifyBucket may mkdir a local dir under the local driver, but that call is already idempotent (recursive:true) and unrelated to a once-only business operation.

guarantee: verifyAccessToken rejects a token signed with any algorithm other than the pinned HS256 (e.g. an HS384 token signed with the same JWT_SECRET throws 'invalid algorithm'), and signAccessToken emits alg=HS256.

guarantee: Document deletion uses a transactional outbox: remove() enqueues a DocumentStorageDeletion row and deletes the document row in one $transaction (deletion record created at order 1, db delete at order 2), and if the db delete throws, storage is never deleted and the outbox row is never marked processed.

guarantee: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes each pending row at most once per success: on success it marks processedAt with lastError/claimedAt cleared (so a redelivery won't re-delete), and on storage failure it increments attempts and clears claimedAt leaving processedAt null so the row is retried safely on a later run rather than double-charged.

guarantee: Concurrent cleanup runs never double-process a deletion: claimPendingStorageDeletions claims rows with a single UPDATE ... SET claimedAt ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED RETURNING ..., bound with the 600000ms stale window and limit, so a row already claimed by another worker within 10 minutes is skipped.
